task: Push the soccer ball into the goal
raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS - A soccer ball is positioned on a flat surface. The ball is spherical and has a textured surface. STRATEGY - The ball must be pushed into the goal. The goal is a rectangular opening in the surface. The ball is not inside the goal initially. The task requires moving the ball from its current position to the goal. Failure modes include pushing the ball too hard and causing it to roll out of the goal or not pushing it far enough to enter the goal. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1: Reach for the ball to establish contact. Step 2: Grasp the ball to control its movement. Step 3: Push the ball towards the goal. Step 4: Ensure the ball enters the goal.**
Goal: inside(soccer_ball, goal)
Scene: soccer_ball, goal, surface
Step 1: reach(soccer_ball)
  affordance_region: the body of the soccer ball
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, soccer_ball, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, soccer_ball) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(soccer_ball)
  affordance_region: the body of the soccer ball
  contact: holding(soccer_ball) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(soccer_ball)
Step 3: push(soccer_ball -> goal)
  affordance_region: the opening of the goal
  contact: surface_contact(soccer_ball, goal) [completion]
  spatial: inside(soccer_ball, goal) [completion]
  direction: push_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: inside(soccer_ball, goal)
<END_OF_PLAN>
